steps.title: Translate the time used per box
steps.content: $x$ large boxes use $12x$ minutes (12 min each). $y$ small boxes use $6y$ minutes (6 min each).

steps.title: Set the total equal to 720
steps.content: The total daily time is 720 minutes: $12x + 6y = 720$.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• $6x+12y=720$: $6x + 12y = 720$: swaps the per-box times (6 min for large, 12 min for small).
  • $12x+6y=720$: $12x + 6y = 720$: correctly multiplies each box count by its own time.
  • $\left(x+y\right)\left(12+6\right)=720$: $(x+y)(12+6)=720$: this would only work if every box took 18 minutes, which is not the case.
  • $\left(12+x\right)\left(6+y\right)=720$: $(12+x)(6+y)=720$: adds counts and times, which is not how time accumulates.
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
